ground_truth.title: Must Read Short Speculative Fiction: November 2025
ground_truth.author: Alex Brown
ground_truth.main_content: Add some of the best new short fiction stories to your end of year reading lists! This month's must-read short speculative fiction recommendations include "Phantom View" by John Wiswell, featuring an illustration of a reddish orange blur resembling a human face peering out from a dusty window pane. Also featured is "Timelike Curves, Spacelike Curves" by P H Lee, illustrated as a person's head tilted back and exploding as it forcefully ejects the fabric of space and time in the form of a femme face. Kate Elliott's "Barnacle" showcases black birds picking at a barnacle-covered rock against a bright red sky. Stephen Graham Jones presents "The Belle of the Ball" with an illustrated recursive image of a man gripping large garden shears as he creeps up behind a couple. These stories represent the best new short fiction available for November 2025.
with: Add some of the best new short fiction stories to your end of year reading lists!
"Timelike Curves, Spacelike Curves" by P H Lee
"The Belle of the Ball" by Stephen Graham Jones
without: Sign up for our weekly newsletter
